task.title: Reject empty stored event-type discriminators explicitly during replay
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/386 by enforcing the non-empty event-type invariant when corrupted or externally modified SQLite rows are replayed.
acceptance_criteria: Replaying a row whose event_type is empty returns ReplayError::InvalidStoredEventType with a stable diagnostic.
The invalid stored event-type error has no underlying source and is returned before typed decoding.
Non-empty unsupported event types retain their existing compatibility classification.
The public documentation describes empty stored discriminators as replay failures.
The pinned local and pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused integration test that replaces a valid persisted discriminator with the empty string and uses a panic-on-call decoder.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test failed to compile because ReplayError had no InvalidStoredEventType variant, proving the stable corruption classification did not yet exist.

attempts.summary: Added an explicit persisted-discriminator guard at the replay boundary.
attempts.patch: Replay now returns ReplayError::InvalidStoredEventType immediately after reading an empty event_type and before reading payload metadata or invoking Event::decode.

attempts.summary: Preserved forward-compatibility behavior while documenting and verifying the corruption boundary.
attempts.patch: The regression test asserts the exact variant, display text, absent source, and decoder bypass; existing non-empty unknown discriminator coverage remained green.

attempts.summary: Completed independent automated review and monitored the required pull-request checks.
attempts.diagnostic: CodeRabbit completed with no actionable comments, Rust quality gate run 29313552539 passed, and pull request 387 squash-merged as fb33246.

outcome.summary: Replay now classifies an empty stored event discriminator as explicit invalid persisted metadata and rejects it before typed decoder dispatch while preserving non-empty compatibility semantics.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test event_log rejects_an_empty_stored_event_type_before_decoding
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29313552539 for pull request 387
CodeRabbit review for pull request 387: review completed with no actionable comments and required status passed
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0016-empty-stored-event-type.json # result: valid development record
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 16 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: Enforce persisted metadata invariants before dispatching into extensible decoder code so corruption classification does not depend on caller behavior.
Use a panic-on-call decoder to prove replay rejects invalid metadata before crossing the typed decoding boundary.
Keep empty discriminators distinct from non-empty unsupported discriminators: the former violate storage invariants, while the latter can represent forward-compatible events.
A unit error variant is sufficient when the invalid persisted domain contains exactly one value and the stable diagnostic names that invariant.
